2604.01221v1,Data format,arXiv: 2604.01221v1	HippoCamp
arXiv
The arXiv identifier for the paper.<SEP>The paper's identifier on the arXiv preprint server is arXiv: 2604.01221v1.

Paper: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers	HippoCamp
Type
The paper's title is "HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers".

controlled setting,environment setup,experiment location	Experiment
HippoCamp
The HippoCamp environment is used as the experimental setup for evaluating contextual agents on personal computers.<SEP>The experiment is conducted in the controlled environment of the HippoCamp setup.

environment configuration,profile isolation	Experimental Setup
HippoCamp
The experimental setup involves isolating profiles within the HippoCamp environment to ensure controlled conditions for evaluation.

access control,file system access	Access
HippoCamp
Agents have restricted access only to their corresponding profile's file system in the HippoCamp environment.

input method,query processing	Agent Mechanism
Natural-Language Query
Agents receive natural-language queries as input and process them using their native mechanisms.

evaluation metric,multimodal corpus access	Factual Retention
HippoCamp
The HippoCamp environment is used to evaluate factual retention by agents accessing a complete multimodal file corpus.

archetypal profiles,overall average	Evaluation Metric
MLLMs and Agent Methods
Representative MLLMs and agent methods are evaluated across various archetypal user profiles and an overall average is reported for factual retention.

evaluation context,multimodal file corpus	Benchmarking Contextual Agents
HippoCamp
The HippoCamp environment serves as the benchmark for evaluating contextual agents' performance on a complete multimodal file corpus.

Hoang-Nhat Nguyen et al.,Kairui Hu,Shuai Liu,Shulin Tian,Zhe Yang,authorship,collaboration	Authors
HippoCamp
The HippoCamp paper was authored by Zhe Yang, Shulin Tian, Kairui Hu, Shuai Liu, and a group of co-authors.<SEP>Zhe Yang, Shulin Tian, Kairui Hu, Shuai Liu, and Hoang-Nhat Nguyen et al. are authors of the paper.

Year
The paper was published in 2026.<SEP>The HippoCamp paper was published in 2026.<SEP>The paper was published in 2026.

Hoang-Nhat Nguyen et al.
Hoang-Nhat Nguyen et al. contributed to the HippoCamp paper and its experiments.<SEP>Hoang-Nhat Nguyen et al. are co-authors of the paper.

method description,methodology	HippoCamp
Method
The method section describes advanced paradigms for grounded reasoning in contextual agents.

assessment process,evaluation framework	Evaluation Protocol
HippoCamp
HippoCamp employs a unified evaluation protocol to assess agent performance on personalized file management tasks.

evaluation method,task type	HippoCamp
Question Answering Evaluation
For question answering tasks, HippoCamp uses an LLM-as-a-judge paradigm for evaluating correctness and quality of responses.

evaluation method,task type	Evidence Retrieval Evaluation
HippoCamp
For evidence retrieval tasks, HippoCamp assesses the accuracy and relevance of retrieved files using recall hit rate and F score.

method family,performance analysis	HippoCamp
RAG Methods
RAG methods are evaluated within the context of HippoCamp's experimental results to highlight their weaknesses in multimodal retrieval and hierarchical reasoning.

method variant,performance evaluation	HippoCamp
Standard RAG
The Standard RAG method is specifically analyzed for its performance on profiling tasks within the HippoCamp framework.

athlete participation,competition	Noah Carter
World Athletics Championship
Noah Carter is competing at the World Athletics Championship.<SEP>Noah Carter is competing at the World Athletics Championship.<SEP>Noah Carter is competing at the World Athletics Championship.
